title: USB-C CC resistor topology needs review

evidence.description: USB-C CC pins and role resistors are reviewed from schematic context

message: USB-C CC resistor topology needs human review.
recommendation: Confirm CC pull-up/down, connector orientation and shield/ESD decisions against the intended USB-C role.
falsePositiveNotes: Waive when the design has no USB-C connector.
impact: USB-C power or orientation behavior can fail despite passing generic ERC.

title: CAN or RS485 protection needs review

evidence.description: Transceiver termination, biasing and ESD assumptions are reviewed

message: CAN/RS485 interface termination and protection need human review.
recommendation: Confirm termination, biasing, common-mode protection and connector pinout for field interfaces.
falsePositiveNotes: Waive when the board has no field bus transceiver.
impact: Field communications can be unreliable or vulnerable to ESD/surge events.

title: I2C pull-up topology needs review

evidence.description: I2C pull-ups and voltage domains are reviewed from schematic context

message: I2C pull-up topology needs human review.
recommendation: Confirm SDA/SCL pull-ups exist and are tied to the expected voltage domain.
falsePositiveNotes: Waive when the design has no I2C bus or pull-ups are provided off-board.
impact: I2C buses can fail bring-up or violate connected device voltage limits.

title: Debug interface accessibility needs review

evidence.description: SWD/JTAG/UART boot and reset access are available through connectors or test points

message: Debug and programming access need human review.
recommendation: Confirm reset, boot mode and debug pins remain accessible after assembly.
falsePositiveNotes: Waive for simple boards with pre-programmed modules or documented factory programming flow.
impact: Firmware bring-up and recovery may require rework if programming access is missing.

title: High-speed differential constraints need review

evidence.description: Differential pair impedance, spacing and length constraints are recorded when high-speed interfaces exist

message: High-speed differential constraints need human review.
recommendation: If the board has USB, Ethernet, MIPI, PCIe or LVDS, confirm net classes and stackup assumptions are documented.
falsePositiveNotes: Waive when the board has no high-speed differential interface.
impact: Signal-integrity assumptions can be invisible to reviewers and manufacturers.
